intro.speaker: Narrator
intro.text: The tunnel opens into a vaulted gallery, strange machines gnawing at veins of pale crystal. A voice calls out of the gloom as the miners notice you.

d1.speaker: Duergar
d1.text: Oi! What are you doing here? This ain't the kind of place to be sniffing around...

f1.speaker: Florian
f1.text: Ah! A person — what a relief! What's all this machinery? You're making such a racket!

d2.speaker: Duergar
d2.text: This? Just a spot of mining. Shouldn't take more than a few weeks.

f2.speaker: Florian
f2.text: (darting between the machines, delighted) Wow! You built these? Incredible. I've never seen technology like it!

d3.speaker: Duergar
d3.text: Thank you, kid — (scoffs). That's my life's work, that is. This beauty can chew through a whole vein of Ironbark faster than a Pale Listener steals a mother. (turning to the others) You lot all right? Taking the boy somewhere safe, I hope?

bark.speaker: Drevok
bark.text: (snickering) Oh, we're minding him very closely. Mind you don't lose him down a shaft — he does wander.

f_herald.speaker: Florian
f_herald.text: Not quite! I am Florian, heir to Bouquet. We come on behalf of Elthiad's guild of adventurers: your operation has gravely upset the local beasts, and many have died for it.

d_bow.speaker: Duergar
d_bow.text: (stepping back) Our apologies, your majesty. We didn't know the crown of flowers — we'd thought Bouquet lost to the flames.

f_frag.speaker: Narrator
f_frag.text: Something in Florian's face falters at that — the proud herald, briefly fragile.

d_explain.speaker: Duergar
d_explain.text: These crystals are vital to our city. We can't simply stop without good reason.

f_name.speaker: Florian
f_name.text: I understand. You lead this work, I take it. What is your name?

k_intro.speaker: Kopmter Coffer
k_intro.text: Aye, that I do. Kopmter Coffer, head of the Coffer family, back in the deep halls of (PLACEHOLDER) —

f_interrupt.speaker: Florian
f_interrupt.text: Wait — you're Duergars! I thought you extinct. Your people fought for my ancestors.

k_proud.speaker: Kopmter Coffer
k_proud.text: Ha! That we did. My great-grandfather, rest him, fought for Thorne and forged half the blades that held the walls.

f_accord.speaker: Florian
f_accord.text: Then I honour your lineage's courage, Kopmter. Let us find a middle path — we were allies once; it would shame both our houses to come to blows.

f_plea.speaker: Florian
f_plea.text: You need these crystals; I won't stand in the way of that — truly, I'd love to learn more. But your methods endanger Elthiad. The Quartz-Mother has left her nest because of the noise.

k_noise.speaker: Kopmter Coffer
k_noise.text: We could only work quieter by giving up the machines — and that we won't. The noise is what keeps us safe down here.

f_branch.speaker: Florian
f_branch.text: (to himself) That's fair. I'd not put them in danger either...

choices.label: I know a spell that could help — though it is costly to maintain.
We could take down the Quartz-Mother together.
What if we found the Quartz-Mother a new home?

opt1.speaker: Kopmter Coffer
opt1.text: A warding to swallow the sound? Hah — we'd need the right components, and we've none to spare here.

choices.label: Then let me travel to your city to gather what's needed.
I'll keep it in mind for now.

opt1_travel.speaker: Kopmter Coffer
opt1_travel.text: ...You'd do that? Aye — come to our halls, and we'll see you have what the rite needs. Bring it back, and the noise need never trouble your beasts again.

opt2.speaker: Kopmter Coffer
opt2.text: For you, your majesty — aye. We'll not wait on the captain's leave for the heir of Bouquet.

opt2_plan.speaker: Florian
opt2_plan.text: Then here's a thought: still the machines and lie in wait at the nest. With the noise gone, she'll come home — and we'll be ready.

choices.label: Set the ambush at the nest.
No — let's track her down instead.

opt2_ambush.speaker: Kopmter Coffer
opt2_ambush.text: A fine plan. Still the machines, and the dark will bring her to you.

opt2_hunter.speaker: Kopmter Coffer
opt2_hunter.text: Then take my best hunter — she knows every tunnel the beast walks. (One of the Duergars shoulders a heavy crossbow and falls in beside you.)

opt3.speaker: Kopmter Coffer
opt3.text: Move her? Hah — and how would you herd six metres of furious quartz-hide across the Deepmark?

opt3_how.speaker: Florian
opt3_how.text: Leave that to me. A lure, perhaps — or a means to speak with her. Only point me to a nest that would suit her.

choices.label: Ask where a new nest might lie.

opt3_nest.speaker: Kopmter Coffer
opt3_nest.text: There's old galleries to the deep east that'd hold her well enough. Lead her there, and we'll trouble each other no more.
